session.name: MPI-64 RunPod Remote Engine — Step 4.2 (create-on-Connect) DONE+verified; Step 4.2.1 (custom-nodes false-PARTIAL fix) DONE+verified; dep-hashes filled; Step 4.3 (stop-not-delete + auto-reconnect + delete-fallback) CODE DONE + LIVE-VERIFIED working (the 'ready but no Pod' was browser cache). NEXT: Step 4.3.3 orphan-Pod sweep + Step 4.3.1 delete-Pod-before-volume-delete (user-picked recommendation), then Step 4.3.2 Disconnect choice popup.

goal.original: Implement MPI-64 RunPod Remote Engine: run ComfyUI generation on a user's RunPod Secure Cloud GPU from Cubric Vision via a Cubric-owned hidden Pod template + token-auth HTTP wrapper, network-volume persistence, app-managed lifecycle, GPU/PyTorch compatibility gates.
goal.summary: Phase 3 Steps 1-4.1 done+verified. This session: Step 4.2 create-on-Connect/delete-on-Disconnect (DONE+verified), Step 4.2.1 custom-nodes false-PARTIAL fix (DONE+verified, model card flips INSTALLED on refresh), dep-hashes filled (9 null sha256), then Step 4.3 RE-REVISED the teardown to STOP-not-delete + auto-reconnect-on-boot with a self-healing delete+recreate fallback (CODE DONE + LIVE-VERIFIED: stale podId → resume host-pinned-fail → auto-recreate fresh Pod 3ukammu6gnsmxh → wrapper ready). The 'ready but no Pod' scare was a RunPod-console browser-cache stale view (Step 4.3.0 closed = not a bug). Live test exposed a REAL orphan-Pod class (Step 4.3.3). NOTHING committed. LIVE BILLING: Pod 3ukammu6gnsmxh is RUNNING (A4000) + an orphan ...vos2 is STOPPED — user should delete both in the RunPod console if not testing.

Step 4.3.1 (NEXT, user-picked, pairs with 4.3.3) — delete attached Pod BEFORE deleting a volume (RunPod refuses volume-delete while a Pod is attached): MpiSettings._confirmDeleteVolume → delete-active + clear podId/wasConnected → DELETE /runpod/volumes/:id
Step 4.3.2 — Disconnect choice popup: Delete Pod vs Keep Pod (warm). Delete → delete-active + clear podId/wasConnected; Keep → stop-active + clear wasConnected (keep podId). Lean Keep as default
Step 4.4 — Persistent connection feedback in status bars (landing 'Local·Offline'→'Remote·Online'+Pod card; gallery 'Idle'→'IDLE·Local / IDLE·Remote')
Step 5 — Manifest compatibility gate + repair/reinitialize UI (Batch #4 fold-in)
Phase 4 — remote input-asset transfer; remote image+video generation end-to-end (NOTE: a real Pod is live now — a generation can be tested immediately)

next_action.description: Continue MPI-64 with the user-picked recommendation: Step 4.3.3 (orphan stopped-Pod sweep) + Step 4.3.1 (delete attached Pod before volume delete), implemented together (both are Pod-cleanup, both prevent stranded billing + a blocked volume delete). 4.3.3: add an account-Pod reconcile — list Pods named 'cubric-vision' via the RunPod client and delete EXITED ones that are not the currently-tracked podId, triggered on Connect/boot (and/or a Settings 'clean up old Pods' action); also make the recreate-fallback in /remote/pod/reconnect delete the SAVED podId being replaced rather than relying on _startedPodId. 4.3.1: in MpiSettings._confirmDeleteVolume, before DELETE /runpod/volumes/:id, first call POST /remote/pod/delete-active and clear runpodConfig.podId+wasConnected, then delete the volume; keep _runpodErrText 'delete the Pod first' as the fallback for a non-tracked attached Pod. Then Step 4.3.2 (Disconnect choice: Delete vs Keep popup). Reuse client.deletePod/listPods? (NOTE: runpodRemote.js exposes createPod/startPod/stopPod/deletePod/getPod but NO list-pods method yet — add a client.listPods that GETs /pods if a sweep needs it; verify the REST shape). Backend lifecycle helpers live in routes/remoteProxy.js (_createPodInternal, _deleteTrackedPod, _isGpuAvailable, _startedPodId, _mode). INLINE-SEQUENTIAL, no sub-agent workers, never commit unless asked. Live Pod ops bill + the classifier BLOCKS reading .secrets/runpod.env and autonomous Pod create/delete — the USER runs live tests. Brief before code (mpi-continue gate).

key_decisions: LIFECYCLE RE-REVISION (user, 2026-06-12): STOP-not-delete on quit/Disconnect REPLACES Step 4.2's delete-on-Disconnect. A STOPPED Pod bills NO GPU (only volume + reserved container disk) and warm-resumes faster than a cold create. DELETE only on GPU-switch, on a failed resume (host-pinned), or when the saved GPU is unavailable. RUNNING bills per-second so it is never left running across quit. The self-healing delete+recreate fallback fixes the host-pinning wall ('not enough free GPUs on the host machine') that originally killed start/stop — a failed resume auto-deletes the stuck Pod and creates fresh on the same GPU.

Step 4.3 endpoints (routes/remoteProxy.js): /remote/pod/create (first Connect + GPU-switch, fresh Pod + new token), /remote/pod/reconnect ({podId,gpuTypeId,volumeId,datacenter} → availability pre-check → if gone delete stuck Pod + {unavailable:true}; else startPod warm-resume same podId+token; on fail delete+createPod fresh new token → {ready,podId,recreated}), /remote/pod/delete-active (GPU-switch/unavailable/explicit), /remote/pod/stop-active (Disconnect+quit teardown — STOP, KEEPS podId+token so boot warm-resumes). main.js quit → stopActiveRemotePod (/remote/pod/stop-active). js/shell.js _initRemoteBoot auto-reconnects when wasConnected+podId+gpuType (background, toasts; unavailable → 'pick another GPU' popup + clears intent; else advisory-only).

wasConnected intent: added to DEFAULT_RUNPOD_CONFIG + normalizeRunpodConfig (storage.js) — set true on a ready Connect/reconnect, cleared on explicit Disconnect. Drives boot auto-reconnect. GPU-switch in Settings deletes the old Pod (delete-active) so the next Connect creates fresh on the new card.

Step 4.2.1 root cause: /comfy/models/check remote branch forwarded custom_nodes deps to the wrapper, which only sees the /workspace volume; custom_nodes ship in the Pod IMAGE (/opt/comfyui/custom_nodes, Design A) → always read missing → pack stuck PARTIAL. The wrapper's _is_complete_on_disk checks file-presence + non-zero size only (sha256 NOT consulted for status) so the null sha256 was NOT the cause. Fix in remoteModels.remoteModelsCheck: type==='custom_nodes' marked installed inline, never sent; per-model installed = AND. installOnEngine is NOT image-resident (4x-NMKD-Siax, yolo/sam .pth are real volume models installed via the wrapper).

Connect branches in MpiSettings._connectEngine: a saved cfg.podId → warm /remote/pod/reconnect ('resuming…'); no podId → /remote/pod/create ('creating…'). data.unavailable → clear podId+wasConnected + 'pick another GPU' hint. data.recreated → 'ready (Pod recreated)'.

MpiButton imperative API (setLabel/setDisabled) lives on instance.el NOT the instance (helpers _engineBtnLabelSet/_engineBtnDisabled call instance.el.setX). MpiDropdown emits 'change' ONLY on a real option click (not on mount/re-render) — so re-rendering the GPU picker does NOT fire the GPU-switch delete.
constraints: LIVE BILLING NOW: Pod 3ukammu6gnsmxh is RUNNING (A4000, EUR-IS-1, volume cfwqthmdrg) and an orphan ...vos2 is STOPPED. If not testing a generation, the user should delete both in the RunPod console (manual until Step 4.3.3 lands). The volume cfwqthmdrg (150GB) bills storage (~$10.50/mo, expected).
NOTHING is committed in Cubric-Vision (never commit unless the user explicitly asks). All MPI-64 app work is uncommitted on branch RunPod. mpi-ci + cubric-remote-wrapper also uncommitted.
INLINE-SEQUENTIAL execution for app code — do not dispatch sub-agent workers.

RunPod secrets + standing IDs in GITIGNORED cubric-remote-wrapper/.secrets/runpod.env: CUBRIC_TOKEN sha8 4bc82fb9, volume cfwqthmdrg, template ejrwtsej15. NEVER paste the API key/token; NEVER read/grep runpod.env (the classifier BLOCKS reading it AND scanning %APPDATA% for the secret store AND autonomous Pod create/delete that needs the key). For live Pod ops the USER runs cubric-remote-wrapper/recreate_pod.py. RunPod is behind Cloudflare — always a browser User-Agent; Windows curl flakes — use Python; RunPod REST stop/delete returns an EMPTY body on success; createPod returns HTTP 201 + {id}.

routes/remoteProxy.js MUST stay mounted BEFORE routes/comfy.js (SSE intercept fall-through). routes/logger.js warn/info take only (category, message). The wrapper token in the app store is keyed by podId (getWrapperToken returns null on podId mismatch) — create/recreate MUST setWrapperToken keyed to the NEW podId; warm resume keeps the same podId so the stored token still matches. The proxy 404s wrapper routes for a few seconds right after Pod ready — remoteModels.wrapperFetch already retries.

PowerShell via Bash is BLOCKED by the classifier (user's PowerShell deny rule) — read app.log with the Read tool (offset/limit), not Get-Content. scripts/new_uuid.py does NOT exist — generate uuids with python -c 'import uuid; print(uuid.uuid4())'.

Generation path (comfyController._ensureRemoteReady) does NOT lazy-create a Pod — it proceeds only if already Connected, else errors 'press Connect'. So a generation test requires an active Connect first (a Pod is live now).
DOCS WEBSITE PUSH BLOCK still applies (never push 'Cubric Studio (Docs)').

IMMEDIATE NEXT (user-picked recommendation): Step 4.3.3 (orphan stopped-Pod sweep — list account Pods named 'cubric-vision' and delete EXITED ones that aren't the tracked podId; recreate-fallback should target the saved podId being replaced) + Step 4.3.1 (delete the attached Pod BEFORE deleting a volume, because RunPod refuses a volume-delete while a Pod is attached — patch MpiSettings._confirmDeleteVolume to delete-active + clear podId/wasConnected, then DELETE the volume), implemented together; then Step 4.3.2 (Disconnect choice popup: Delete Pod vs Keep Pod warm).
